name: Check Into Cash
description_short: Check Into Cash Elmwood Park, Illinois — Check Into Cash offers payday loans up to $600, title loans, and check cashing services with same-day funding a...
description_long: Check Into Cash has operated for 30 years as a storefront-based lender specializing in short-term financial solutions. The company operates a chain of physical locations (including the Ferndale, MI location detailed here) where customers can apply in person for quick cash. Their primary business model centers on payday and title loans—products designed for consumers facing unexpected expenses who need funds quickly. The company also offers installment loans, a flex line of credit, and ancillary services like check cashing, bill pay, and Western Union transfers. Check Into Cash's model relies on rapid approval and same-day cash disbursement, positioning themselves as an alternative to traditional banking for emergency situations. What distinguishes Check Into Cash is their established retail footprint and 30-year operational history, combined with multiple loan product options beyond basic payday loans. They emphasize convenience through both in-store and online application pathways, and offer related financial services (check cashing, bill pay, tax preparation) in a single location. However, like all payday and title lenders, Check Into Cash operates in a high-cost lending category. Payday and title loans typically carry APRs exceeding 300-400% and create debt cycles for vulnerable borrowers. While the company serves a real market need for same-day emergency funds, these products are fundamentally more expensive than alternatives like credit union PALs, employer advances, or personal loans from banks. The lack of specific APR or fee information on the location page—with customers directed to "see store for details"—is characteristic of the industry's opacity around true borrowing costs.
address: 6816 W North Ave, Elmwood Park, IL 60707

company_info.headquarters: 6816 W North Ave, Elmwood Park, IL 60707

pros: Same-day funding available: customers can walk out with cash in hand if approved
Multiple loan types offered: payday loans up to $600, installment loans, title loans, and flex line of credit provide options beyond basic payday loans
Established 30-year track record with 200+ locations nationwide provides operational stability
Convenient application: accepts applications online or in-store with minimal documentation (ID, proof of income, bank account info)
Additional financial services under one roof: check cashing, bill pay, Western Union, tax preparation, and Green Dot debit cards
Extended store hours: Ferndale location open 8am-7pm weekdays, 9am-4pm Saturday for working customers
No credit score requirement mentioned: focuses on ability to repay via income verification rather than credit history
cons: Payday and title loans carry extremely high APRs (300-400%+ typical industry range), creating significant debt trap risk for repeat borrowers
Specific fees and rates not disclosed on website; customers must visit store or call for pricing, limiting transparency in decision-making
Title loans require collateral (vehicle title), risking car repossession if borrower cannot repay
Short repayment windows on payday loans create cash flow problems if borrowers cannot repay on next paycheck
Limited to $600 maximum for payday loans, excluding customers needing larger emergency amounts
best_for: Workers with stable income facing true emergencies (car repair, medical bill) who need funds within 24 hours and have no other options
Customers with poor or no credit history who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans or credit union products
People who need check cashing, bill pay, or money transfer services and want to combine those with a loan application
diagnosis: Check Into Cash is best for employed individuals facing genuine emergencies who need cash within hours and have exhausted alternatives like credit unions, employer advances, or bank personal loans. The critical caveat is that payday and title loans are extremely expensive emergency products (300%+ APR typical) that create debt cycles for many borrowers; they should only be used as a true last resort, never for non-emergency expenses.
services: Installment loans with extended repayment terms
Title loans using vehicle title as collateral
Flex line of credit with revolving access
Check cashing (including checks others won't accept)
Green Dot Visa debit card accounts
